title: relay/store: drain() DELETE uses id <= last_id — silently drops messages if no batch found
description: drain() (relay/store.rs line 743) deletes by WHERE id <= last_id OR expires_at <= now. When rows is empty, rows.last() is None and the bind is i64::MAX, which deletes ALL expired messages but no live ones. This is correct but the i64::MAX default is non-obvious — a reader who does not trace through the None case will think the DELETE is a no-op when the result is empty. The intent should be made explicit: when rows is empty, only delete expired messages (use a separate DELETE or explicitly comment why i64::MAX is correct). The current approach works but will confuse the next maintainer who touches this code.
